response.justification: Bogdan presents a credible Staff-level IC trajectory with genuine AI-native experience in his current role — MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure are directly relevant. His time at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic demonstrates real high-throughput distributed systems depth (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O). However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from the resume, which is a material gap. His distributed systems work is also 6+ years old, with more recent roles focused on product engineering and developer tooling. Stakeholder management and incident-response ownership are not substantiated.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP-based agentic workflows — genuine AI-native engineering context
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and low-latency design
Consistent Staff-level IC positioning across multiple organizations, with evidence of technical leadership, TDD advocacy, and cross-language systems breadth
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills list despite being a stated must-have technical skill for the role
High-throughput distributed systems experience is concentrated in 2018–2020; more recent work skews toward product engineering and developer tooling rather than reliability-critical infrastructure
No explicit evidence of on-call ownership, incident response, or formal cross-functional stakeholder management at the scale the role requires
